body:
### Problem

Neovim ui2 crashes with SIGABRT when, *from what I've tested so far*, the result from  Ex command shell redirection that exceeds 4096 bytes is attempted to be displayed in the temporary buffer. For example: , `:!find`, `:!cat file`, `:w !cat` returning a fair volume of output.

Discovered when the results of my `:!find` happened to match the bug trigger conditions.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d8b5ab38-6571-452c-9caa-7d294488ed2e



### Steps to reproduce

Minimal:
```sh
nvim --clean \
	+'lua require("vim._core.ui2").enable({ enabled = true, msg = { targets = "msg" } })' \
	+'norm yy4097p' \
	+'w !cat'
```

1. Enable ui2 with message window
2. Add 4096+1 blank lines
3. Pipe into `cat` and display output in ui2 temp buffer

Alternative:
```sh
nvim --clean \
	+'lua require("vim._core.ui2").enable({ enabled = true, msg = { targets = "msg" } })' \
	+'norm 40ii' \
	+'norm yy99p' \
	+'w !cat'{,}
```

1. Enable it
2. Fill the buffer with 4096+4 (100row * 41col) characters
3. Pipe into `cat` twice in succession

Logs:
<details>
<summary>nvim.log</summary>

</details>

### Expected behavior

No crash

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

0.12, 0.13

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

N/A

### Operating system/version

Ubuntu, Termux

### Terminal name/version

Raw TTY, Ghostty, Termux

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-256color, linux

### Installation

compiled, appimage, termux-packages

labels.description: Nvim 0.12 mesages/dialog/pager/notifications based on the UI-ext protocol
labels.name: ui2

title: ui2 with message window crashes when message size exceeds 4KiB
